title: Oldenlandia auricularia
taxonomy: Oldenlandia auricularia is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant is also classified within the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plant families such as Apocynaceae and Rubiaceae. Within the order Gentianales, Oldenlandia auricularia is further classified as a member of the clade Gentianidae. The species Oldenlandia auricularia is also classified within the tribe Oldenlandieae, which is a group of flowering plants within the family Rubiaceae. The tribe Oldenlandieae is further classified within the subfamily Rubiaceae.
uses: Oldenlandia auricularia is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa and Asia. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In addition, Oldenlandia auricularia is thought to have antioxidant and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for certain diseases. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

intro: Oldenlandia auricularia, also known as the ear drop or earlobe plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia auricularia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oldenlandia auricularia is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or extract, and is often used in traditional remedies for colds, coughs, and fever, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
